## 9. Concurrency, Thread-Safety, Edge Cases & Extensibility

**DEFAULT: describe verbally. Write the thread-safe code only if asked.**

üó£Ô∏è **Step 1 ‚Äî the exact race:** "It's in `park`. Two cars request the last fitting spot at once. Both `select` returns that spot, both `occupy` it, both get tickets ‚Äî double-assigned. Classic check-then-act: the check and the act aren't one atomic step."

üó£Ô∏è **Step 2 ‚Äî the fix:** "Put a lock around the select-and-occupy block. The first car in holds it through both; the second waits, then re-runs `select`, sees the spot gone, and gets a clean `LotFullError`."

üó£Ô∏è **Step 3 ‚Äî trade-off:** "I lock LOT-WIDE because nearest-first allocation scans across floors ‚Äî the decision spans floors, so the critical section must too. It serialises parking, but parking is fast ‚Äî find + mark. If I needed more throughput I'd partition allocation per floor and lock per floor, so different floors park in parallel."

üó£Ô∏è **Step 4 ‚Äî distributed:** "Across many servers an in-process lock only protects one process. I'd push atomicity to the DB: `UPDATE spot SET status='OCCUPIED' WHERE id=? AND status='AVAILABLE'` ‚Äî a compare-and-set; whoever's update affects 0 rows lost and retries or gets the typed error."

### Concurrency vocabulary (be fluent)

| Term | Plain meaning | Where it appears here |
|---|---|---|
| **Race condition** | two threads touch shared state, result depends on timing | two cars for the last spot |
| **Critical section** | the block that must run atomically | select-and-occupy in `park` |
| **Check-then-act** | check a condition, act on it, but it changed in between | "saw the spot free, then occupied it" |
| **Atomic** | all-or-nothing, no half-done state observed | what the lock guarantees |
| **Mutex / lock** | a token only one thread holds at a time | the lot-wide `Lock` |
| **Lock granularity** | how much one lock covers ‚Äî global vs per-resource | lot-wide here (allocation spans floors) |
| **Pessimistic locking** | grab the lock before touching state | the in-process approach |
| **Optimistic locking** | don't lock; write conditionally, retry if you lost | the distributed DB compare-and-set |
| **Compare-and-set** | atomic "update only if still X" | `WHERE status='AVAILABLE'` |
| **Deadlock** | two threads each hold a lock the other needs | avoided ‚Äî one lock, held briefly |

üéôÔ∏è Fluent sentence: "This is a check-then-act race in the critical section; I take a pessimistic lot-wide mutex because allocation spans floors, and for the distributed case I switch to optimistic locking with a compare-and-set."

### Concurrency follow-up Q&A

> ‚ùì "Why a plain `Lock` and not an `RLock`?" ‚Üí "No guarded method here re-enters its own lock ‚Äî `park` and `unpark` each take the lock once and never call another locked method while holding it. A plain mutex is the minimal correct tool. I'd switch to `RLock` only if a guarded helper were called by a guarded method."

> ‚ùì "Why lot-wide and not per-floor?" ‚Üí "Because nearest-first allocation scans across floors ‚Äî the decision is global, so the select+occupy must be atomic across the whole lot. A per-floor lock wouldn't stop two threads both choosing the same nearest spot. If the design let drivers pick a floor, I'd lock per floor and they'd run in parallel."

> ‚ùì "Doesn't a lot-wide lock kill throughput?" ‚Üí "The critical section is just a scan plus a status flip ‚Äî microseconds ‚Äî so contention is low for a normal lot. If profiling showed the lock hot, I'd shard allocation per floor with per-floor locks, or move to the DB compare-and-set so spots book independently."

> ‚ùì "Pessimistic or optimistic ‚Äî which and why?" ‚Üí "Pessimistic in-process: tiny critical section, low contention, no retry loop needed. Distributed, I flip to optimistic ‚Äî you can't hold a Python lock across servers, so a conditional DB write plus retry is the idiomatic atomic primitive."

> ‚ùì "What if the process crashes after `occupy` but before registering the ticket?" ‚Üí "In-process the crash takes the whole process down, so the half state is gone with it. For the distributed version I'd wrap occupy + ticket-insert in one DB transaction so a crash rolls back both."

### ‚ùì IF ASKED ‚Äî the thread-safe version

**Part A ‚Äî the no-op hook + subclass:**
```python copy
# clean core: does nothing
@contextmanager
def _lock(self):
    yield

# thread-safe: override ONLY this
class ThreadSafeParkingService(ParkingService):
    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        self._mutex = threading.Lock()

    @contextmanager
    def _lock(self):
        # plain Lock ‚Äî nothing re-enters; lot-wide because allocation scans floors
        with self._mutex:
            yield
```
- **Plain Lock, not RLock:** nothing re-enters its own lock ‚Äî minimal correct tool.
- **Lot-wide, not per-floor:** allocation scans across floors, so the select+occupy must be atomic lot-wide.

**Part B ‚Äî the exact modified method:** the `park` body is **byte-for-byte identical** to ¬ß6 ‚Äî `with self._lock()` simply becomes a real lot mutex via the subclass. The clean code never learns about threads.

üéôÔ∏è "Zero changes to the method. I override one hook ‚Äî that's the Template Method payoff."

**Part C ‚Äî proof:** `test_last_spot_race_exactly_one_winner` ‚Äî a `Barrier(50)` makes all threads hit `park` for one spot at one instant; exactly 1 ticket, 49 typed `LotFullError`, the spot OCCUPIED. `test_concurrent_parks_no_spot_double_assigned` additionally proves no spot is assigned to two vehicles.

### Hardest follow-up questions (concurrency / distribution / scale)

> ‚ùì "Multiple entry gates on different servers ‚Äî your lock is useless." ‚Üí "Right, it only guards one process. # Python 3.11+
"""The orchestrator. Owns active tickets and coordinates park / unpark.

CLEAN, single-threaded-correct core. No locks in this file ‚Äî the only concurrency
hook is the no-op `_lock`, which the thread-safe subclass overrides.

Allocation spans floors (nearest-first scans every floor), so the critical
section is LOT-WIDE: the lock covers the whole select-and-occupy step.
"""

import itertools
import threading
from collections.abc import Iterator
from contextlib import contextmanager
from datetime import datetime

from models import (
    LotFullError,
    ParkingFloor,
    SpotSize,
    Ticket,
    TicketAlreadyClosedError,
    TicketStatus,
    UnknownTicketError,
    Vehicle,
)
from strategies import AllocationStrategy, FeeStrategy


class ParkingService:
    """The brain. Every park/unpark flows through here; it owns the ticket
    registry so it always holds the truth about what's parked.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        floors: list[ParkingFloor],
        allocation: AllocationStrategy,
        fee_strategy: FeeStrategy,
        now_fn=datetime.now,
    ) -> None:
        self.floors = floors
        self.allocation = allocation
        self.fee_strategy = fee_strategy
        self._now = now_fn                              # inject the clock -> testable fees
        self._tickets: dict[str, Ticket] = {}           # ticket_id -> Ticket
        self._counter = itertools.count(1)

    # ‚îÄ‚îÄ concurrency hook (Template Method) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
    @contextmanager
    def _lock(self) -> Iterator[None]:
        # Does NOTHING here. The thread-safe subclass overrides this with a real
        # lot-wide lock. Lot-wide because allocation scans across all floors.
        yield

    # ‚îÄ‚îÄ operations ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
    def park(self, vehicle: Vehicle) -> Ticket:
        """Find a fitting spot, occupy it, and issue a ticket ‚Äî atomically."""
        required = vehicle.required_size

        with self._lock():
            # select + occupy is check-then-act -> must be one atomic step.
            spot = self.allocation.select(self.floors, required)
            if spot is None:
                raise LotFullError(f"no spot available for {vehicle.type.name}")
            spot.occupy()                               # AVAILABLE -> OCCUPIED
            ticket = Ticket(
                id=f"TKT-{next(self._counter)}",
                spot_id=spot.id,
                floor=spot.floor,
                vehicle=vehicle,
                entry_time=self._now(),
            )
            self._tickets[ticket.id] = ticket

        return ticket

    def unpark(self, ticket_id: str) -> Ticket:
        """Close a ticket, compute the fee, and free the spot."""
        ticket = self._tickets.get(ticket_id)
        if ticket is None:
            raise UnknownTicketError(ticket_id)
        if ticket.status == TicketStatus.CLOSED:
            raise TicketAlreadyClosedError(f"ticket {ticket_id} already closed")

        with self._lock():
            spot = self._find_spot(ticket.floor, ticket.spot_id)
            spot.release()                              # OCCUPIED -> AVAILABLE
            ticket.exit_time = self._now()
            ticket.fee = self.fee_strategy.compute(ticket.entry_time, ticket.exit_time)
            ticket.status = TicketStatus.CLOSED

        return ticket

    def availability(self) -> dict[SpotSize, int]:
        """Count free spots by size ‚Äî what a 'spaces available' sign would show."""
        counts: dict[SpotSize, int] = {size: 0 for size in SpotSize}
        for floor in self.floors:
            for spot in floor.spots.values():
                if spot.is_available():
                    counts[spot.size] += 1
        return counts

    def get_ticket(self, ticket_id: str) -> Ticket:
        ticket = self._tickets.get(ticket_id)
        if ticket is None:
            raise UnknownTicketError(ticket_id)
        return ticket

    # ‚îÄ‚îÄ helpers ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
    def _find_spot(self, floor_id: int, spot_id: str):
        for floor in self.floors:
            if floor.id == floor_id:
                return floor.spots[spot_id]
        raise UnknownTicketError(f"spot {spot_id} not found on floor {floor_id}")


class ThreadSafeParkingService(ParkingService):
    """Same logic as the clean core; the ONLY change is a real lot-wide lock.
    The diff from the clean version is one overridden method.
    """

    def __init__(self, *args: object, **kwargs: object) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        self._mutex = threading.Lock()

    @contextmanager
    def _lock(self) -> Iterator[None]:
        # A plain Lock is enough ‚Äî no guarded method re-enters its own lock here.
        # Lot-wide (not per-floor) because nearest-first allocation scans every
        # floor, so the select+occupy must be atomic across the whole lot.
        with self._mutex:
            yield

"""Narrated demo driver: park/unpark, best-fit allocation, fee on exit, boundary
errors, and a 50-thread race for a single last spot. Run: python3 main.py
"""

import threading
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

from models import (
    LotFullError,
    ParkingFloor,
    ParkingSpot,
    SpotSize,
    SpotStatus,
    TicketAlreadyClosedError,
    TicketStatus,
    UnknownTicketError,
    Vehicle,
    VehicleType,
)
from parking_service import ParkingService, ThreadSafeParkingService
from strategies import BestFitAllocation, HourlyFeeStrategy, NearestFirstAllocation


def build_floor(floor_id: int, layout: dict[SpotSize, int]) -> ParkingFloor:
    spots: dict[str, ParkingSpot] = {}
    n = 0
    for size, count in layout.items():
        for _ in range(count):
            n += 1
            sid = f"F{floor_id}-{size.name[0]}{n}"
            spots[sid] = ParkingSpot(id=sid, floor=floor_id, size=size)
    return ParkingFloor(id=floor_id, spots=spots)


def fresh_service(clock: dict, allocation=None) -> ParkingService:
    floors = [
        build_floor(1, {SpotSize.SMALL: 1, SpotSize.MEDIUM: 1, SpotSize.LARGE: 1}),
        build_floor(2, {SpotSize.MEDIUM: 2}),
    ]
    return ParkingService(
        floors,
        allocation or BestFitAllocation(),
        HourlyFeeStrategy(rate_per_hour=2.0),
        now_fn=lambda: clock["t"],
    )


def main() -> None:
    clock = {"t": datetime(2026, 7, 1, 9, 0, 0)}

    # ‚îÄ‚îÄ Case 1: best-fit ‚Äî a motorcycle takes the SMALL spot, not a bigger one ‚îÄ‚îÄ
    svc = fresh_service(clock)
    t1 = svc.park(Vehicle("M-1", VehicleType.MOTORCYCLE))
    assert t1.spot_id.startswith("F1-S"), f"best-fit should pick SMALL, got {t1.spot_id}"
    print("Case 1 best-fit picks SMALL for motorcycle -> OK")

    # ‚îÄ‚îÄ Case 2: a truck needs a LARGE spot ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
    t2 = svc.park(Vehicle("T-1", VehicleType.TRUCK))
    assert t2.spot_id.startswith("F1-L")
    print("Case 2 truck gets LARGE spot -> OK")

    # ‚îÄ‚îÄ Case 3: fee computed on exit (injected clock, 90 min -> 2 hours) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
    clock["t"] = clock["t"] + timedelta(minutes=90)
    closed = svc.unpark(t1.id)
    assert closed.status == TicketStatus.CLOSED
    assert closed.fee == 4.0, f"90 min at $2/started-hour = $4, got {closed.fee}"
    print("Case 3 fee on exit: 90 min -> $4 -> OK")

    # ‚îÄ‚îÄ Case 4: freed spot is reusable ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
    t3 = svc.park(Vehicle("M-2", VehicleType.MOTORCYCLE))
    assert t3.spot_id.startswith("F1-S"), "freed SMALL spot should be reused"
    print("Case 4 freed spot reused -> OK")

    # ‚îÄ‚îÄ Case 5: lot full for a size -> typed rejection ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
    # 3 MEDIUM spots + 1 LARGE all fit a car (4 cars park); the 5th has nothing left.
    svc5 = fresh_service(clock)
    cars = [svc5.park(Vehicle(f"C-{i}", VehicleType.CAR)) for i in range(4)]
    assert len(cars) == 4   # 3 mediums + 1 large
    try:
        svc5.park(Vehicle("C-X", VehicleType.CAR))
        assert False
    except LotFullError:
        print("Case 5 lot full for CARs -> LotFullError -> OK")

    # ‚îÄ‚îÄ Case 6: boundary errors are all typed ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
    for label, fn in [
        ("unknown ticket", lambda: svc.unpark("TKT-999")),
        ("double unpark", lambda: svc.unpark(t1.id)),
        ("unknown ticket get", lambda: svc.get_ticket("nope")),
    ]:
        try:

    floors = [build_floor(1, {SpotSize.MEDIUM: 1})]   # exactly ONE medium spot

    threads = [threading.Thread(target=attempt, args=(i,)) for i in range(N)]
    for t in threads:
        t.start()
    for t in threads:

    assert len(wins) == 1, f"expected exactly 1 winner, got {len(wins)}"
    assert len(losses) == N - 1
    only_spot = floors[0].spots["F1-M1"]
    assert only_spot.status == SpotStatus.OCCUPIED
    print(f"Case 7 concurrency: 1 parked, {len(losses)} rejected, spot taken once -> OK")

    print("\nALL CASES PASSED")

Covers every public operation, every typed error path, both allocation

import pytest

)

)

    spots: dict[str, ParkingSpot] = {}

    for size, count in layout.items():

            n += 1
            sid = f"F{floor_id}-{size.name[0]}{n}"
            spots[sid] = ParkingSpot(id=sid, floor=floor_id, size=size)
    return ParkingFloor(id=floor_id, spots=spots)

    floors = [_floor(1, {SpotSize.MEDIUM: 1})]   # ONE spot
    tsvc = ThreadSafeParkingService(
        floors, NearestFirstAllocation(), HourlyFeeStrategy(),
